name: Sundowners Bar & Grill
description: Iconic West End waterfront restaurant serving fresh seafood and Caribbean favorites. Best sunset views on the island.
insiderTip: Grab a dock seat by 4:30pm for sunset.
addressDescription: Main road in West End, on the water

name: Cannibal Cafe
description: Beloved West End breakfast and lunch spot with strong coffee and hearty plates. A backpacker institution.
insiderTip: The banana pancakes are legendary.
addressDescription: Half Moon Bay road, West End

name: Roatan Oasis
description: Upscale beachfront dining in West Bay with international cuisine and craft cocktails. Perfect for a special night out.
insiderTip: Reserve a table on the sand for dinner.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, near the south end

name: Baleadas Express
description: Authentic Honduran baleadas made fresh to order. The best local street food on the west side of the island.
insiderTip: Ask for extra mantequilla and hot sauce.
addressDescription: Near the main intersection in Coxen Hole

name: Lighthouse Restaurant
description: Elevated seafood dining at Roatan's most iconic hilltop location. Panoramic views of the Caribbean and West End.
insiderTip: Go for lunch to enjoy the view in daylight.
addressDescription: Hilltop above Sandy Bay, follow signs from main road

name: Cafe Escondido
description: Hidden gem cafe in French Harbour with the best espresso on the island. Great pastries baked fresh daily.
insiderTip: Try the coconut latte.
addressDescription: Behind the French Harbour plaza

name: Thai Food Roatan
description: Surprisingly authentic Thai cuisine in West End. Run by a Thai-Honduran couple using imported spices.
insiderTip: The green curry is the best dish.
addressDescription: Main road West End, across from the church

name: Hibiscus Kitchen
description: Farm-to-table restaurant using ingredients from their own garden. Creative Caribbean fusion dishes.
insiderTip: The daily special is always the best choice.
addressDescription: Sandy Bay main road, look for the yellow building

name: Pizza al Paso
description: Wood-fired pizza with Caribbean toppings like jerk chicken and plantain. Quick, delicious, and affordable.
insiderTip: Order the garlic knots as a starter.
addressDescription: West End main strip near the dive shops

name: Oak Ridge Seafood Shack
description: Fresh-off-the-boat seafood at rock-bottom prices. A local favorite far from the tourist crowds.
insiderTip: Ask what came in on the boat that morning.
addressDescription: Oak Ridge waterfront, near the bridge

name: Blue Bahia Beach Bar
description: Laidback beach bar right on the sand in West Bay. Cold beers, rum punches, and zero stress.
insiderTip: Happy hour is 3-5pm with half-price cocktails.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, south section

name: West End Divers Bar
description: The unofficial headquarters for dive culture on Roatan. Cheap drinks, dive stories, and new friends.
insiderTip: Wednesday is quiz night.
addressDescription: West End waterfront, near the dock

name: Land's End Bar
description: Perched on the rocks at the tip of West Bay with infinity pool views. Upscale cocktails in a stunning setting.
insiderTip: Come at sunset for the most dramatic views.
addressDescription: Far south end of West Bay Beach

name: Eagle Ray Bar
description: Funky over-the-water bar with swings instead of stools. Great rum selection and chill reggae vibes.
insiderTip: Sit on the swings over the water.
addressDescription: North end of West End, over the water

name: Jolly Roger Bar
description: Pirate-themed bar on the waterfront with strong drinks and a party atmosphere every weekend.
insiderTip: Friday night is the biggest party in West End.
addressDescription: West End main strip, impossible to miss

name: Sandy Bay Brewing Co.
description: Roatan's first craft brewery serving house-made ales and lagers. Great fish tacos too.
insiderTip: Try the Island Blonde ale.
addressDescription: Sandy Bay main road

name: Coconut Bar
description: No-frills local watering hole in Coxen Hole. Cold Salva Vida beers and loud music.
insiderTip: Cheapest beers on the island.
addressDescription: Downtown Coxen Hole, near the market

name: French Harbour Wine Bar
description: Intimate wine bar with an impressive selection of South American and European wines. Cheese boards available.
insiderTip: Ask for the reserve wine list.
addressDescription: French Harbour plaza, second floor

name: Tranquil Seas Bar
description: Quiet rooftop bar with views of the harbour. Great place to wind down after a day of exploring.
insiderTip: The mojitos are made with fresh island mint.
addressDescription: Above the marina in French Harbour

name: Coconut Tree Divers
description: Top-rated PADI 5-star dive center in West End. Small groups, expert guides, and amazing reef sites.
insiderTip: Book the afternoon 2-tank dive for fewer crowds.
addressDescription: West End waterfront, main dock area

name: West Bay Divers
description: Boutique dive shop steps from West Bay Beach. Specializes in discover scuba and open water certifications.
insiderTip: Their house reef has seahorses.
addressDescription: North end of West Bay Beach

name: Roatan Divers
description: One of the oldest dive operations on the island. Daily trips to the best wall dives on the north side.
insiderTip: Request the Mary's Place dive site.
addressDescription: Sandy Bay waterfront

name: Reef Gliders
description: Eco-conscious dive center focused on reef conservation. Free reef ecology briefings before every dive.
insiderTip: Join their weekly reef cleanup dive for free.
addressDescription: South end of West End

name: Island Snorkel Tours
description: Guided snorkel trips to the best shallow reefs around West Bay. No experience needed, gear included.
insiderTip: The coral gardens tour is best for kids.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, near the main entrance

name: French Harbour Dive Center
description: Less crowded dive sites on the south side of the island. Great for experienced divers wanting solitude.
insiderTip: Ask about the wreck dive.
addressDescription: French Harbour marina

name: Oak Ridge Snorkel Adventure
description: Boat-based snorkel tours through the mangrove tunnels and pristine reefs near Oak Ridge.
insiderTip: The mangrove tunnel tour is unique to this area.
addressDescription: Oak Ridge dock, east side

name: Deep Blue Freediving
description: Professional freediving school offering courses from beginner to instructor level. Warm Caribbean waters year-round.
insiderTip: The intro course is only 2 hours.
addressDescription: West End, near Half Moon Bay

name: Submarine Dive Center
description: Full-service dive center with their own boat fleet. Offers everything from discover scuba to divemaster courses.
insiderTip: Multi-day packages save you 20%.
addressDescription: West Bay, near Infinity Bay

name: Roatan Zipline Adventure
description: 22-cable zipline course through the jungle canopy with ocean views. The longest zipline in Central America.
insiderTip: Go in the morning when it's cooler.
addressDescription: Hilltop above Sandy Bay, shuttle provided

name: Captain Van's Island Tours
description: Full-day island tours hitting all the highlights. Knowledgeable local guides and comfortable transport.
insiderTip: The east end tour includes a free lunch.
addressDescription: Pickup at Coxen Hole cruise port

name: Roatan Fishing Charters
description: Deep sea and reef fishing with experienced captains. Catch marlin, mahi-mahi, wahoo, and more.
insiderTip: Half-day morning trips have the best bite.
addressDescription: West End dock

name: Mangrove Kayak Tours
description: Guided kayak trips through Roatan's mangrove channels. See birds, fish, and untouched nature.
insiderTip: Bring waterproof sunscreen and a hat.
addressDescription: Oak Ridge waterfront, meet at the blue dock

name: Gumbalimba Park
description: Nature park with monkeys, macaws, hanging bridges, and a private beach. Fun for all ages.
insiderTip: The monkeys are most active in the morning.
addressDescription: Road to West Bay, well-signed entrance

name: Glass Bottom Boat Tours
description: See the reef without getting wet. Perfect for non-swimmers and young children.
insiderTip: The 2pm tour has the best light for photos.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, main dock

name: Horseback Riding Roatan
description: Beach and jungle horseback rides with friendly island horses. No experience necessary.
insiderTip: The sunset beach ride is magical.
addressDescription: Sandy Bay, near Carambola Gardens

name: Island Chocolate Factory
description: Tour a working chocolate factory and taste Honduran cacao products. Small-batch artisan chocolate.
insiderTip: Buy the sea salt chocolate bar as a souvenir.
addressDescription: West End, behind the main strip

name: Punta Gorda Cultural Tour
description: Visit the Garifuna community of Punta Gorda. Experience traditional music, dance, and food.
insiderTip: Try the machuca and hudut dishes.
addressDescription: Meet at the Punta Gorda community center

name: West Bay Gift Shop
description: Curated souvenirs and island art. Higher quality than the cruise port shops.
insiderTip: The hand-carved wooden dolphins are locally made.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, near the main entrance

name: Roatan Art Gallery
description: Local and expat artists showcasing Caribbean-inspired paintings, sculpture, and photography.
insiderTip: Ask about the rotating exhibitions.
addressDescription: West End main road, upstairs

name: Island Pearl Jewelry
description: Handcrafted jewelry using local pearls and Caribbean gemstones. Unique pieces you won't find elsewhere.
insiderTip: The pink conch pearl pieces are stunning.
addressDescription: French Harbour plaza, ground floor

name: Coxen Hole Municipal Market
description: The main local market with produce, spices, and everyday goods. The real Roatan experience.
insiderTip: Saturday morning has the freshest selection.
addressDescription: Downtown Coxen Hole, main square

name: Driftwood Designs
description: Boutique shop selling handmade home decor from reclaimed island materials. Beautiful driftwood pieces.
insiderTip: They ship internationally if you find something big.
addressDescription: West End, near the bridge

name: Mahogany Bay Shopping Village
description: Collection of shops at the cruise port. Convenient but touristy. Good for last-minute souvenirs.
insiderTip: Prices are higher here than in town.
addressDescription: Mahogany Bay cruise port

name: Island Surf & Board Shop
description: Surf gear, rash guards, reef-safe sunscreen, and water sports accessories.
insiderTip: They have the best reef-safe sunscreen selection.
addressDescription: West End main strip

name: Caribbean Cigar Co.
description: Hand-rolled Honduran cigars and a comfortable lounge. Great selection from local tobacco farms.
insiderTip: The house blend is excellent value.
addressDescription: West Bay, near the resorts

name: Infinity Bay Resort
description: Beachfront resort on West Bay with infinity pool and full-service spa. The premium West Bay experience.
insiderTip: Book an upper floor room for the best views.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, center section

name: Mayan Princess Resort
description: All-inclusive beachfront resort with spacious condos on West Bay Beach. Great for families.
insiderTip: The ground floor units have direct beach access.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, south end

name: Posada Arco Iris
description: Colorful budget hostel in the heart of West End. Dorms and private rooms with a social atmosphere.
insiderTip: The rooftop hammocks are the best chill spot.
addressDescription: West End main road, near the church

name: Casa Calico
description: Charming boutique B&B in Sandy Bay with beautiful gardens and ocean views. Peaceful and quiet.
insiderTip: Breakfast on the terrace is included.
addressDescription: Sandy Bay hillside, follow signs from main road

name: Turquoise Bay Dive Resort
description: Dive-focused resort with unlimited shore diving included. Popular with serious divers.
insiderTip: The house reef is one of the best on the island.
addressDescription: Sandy Bay waterfront

name: Luna Beach Cabanas
description: Simple beachfront cabanas in a quiet stretch of West End. No frills, just sand and sea.
insiderTip: Cabana 3 has the best ocean view.
addressDescription: North end of West End beach

name: Harbour View Hotel
description: Business-class hotel in French Harbour. Clean rooms, reliable WiFi, and easy access to the marina.
insiderTip: Ask for a harbour-facing room.
addressDescription: French Harbour main road, near the marina

name: Camp Bay Eco Lodge
description: Off-grid eco lodge on Roatan's remote east end. Solar powered with pristine beach access.
insiderTip: Disconnect completely - there's no WiFi by design.
addressDescription: Camp Bay, end of the road

name: Port Royal Plantation Inn
description: Historic inn near the old British fort ruins. A unique stay for history lovers.
insiderTip: Explore the fort ruins at sunset.
addressDescription: Port Royal, near the fort ruins

name: Island Scooter Rentals
description: Reliable scooters for exploring the island. Helmets included, free delivery to West Bay and West End.
insiderTip: Fill up at the gas station in Coxen Hole, it's cheapest.
addressDescription: West End main road, near the entrance

name: Roatan Car Rental
description: Reliable cars, SUVs, and 4x4s for island exploration. Airport and hotel pickup available.
insiderTip: Get a 4x4 if you plan to go to the east end.
addressDescription: Near Coxen Hole airport

name: West Bay Kayak Rental
description: Single and double kayaks for exploring West Bay coastline. Paddleboards also available.
insiderTip: Paddle south to the rocky point for great snorkeling.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, near the palapa

name: Captain Bob's Boat Rental
description: Rent a panga or small motorboat to explore Roatan's coastline at your own pace. Fuel included.
insiderTip: Ask Captain Bob for his secret beach recommendations.
addressDescription: West End dock area

name: Sandy Bay Golf Cart Rentals
description: Golf carts are the fun way to get around the west end of the island. Easy to drive, no license needed.
insiderTip: Reserve a day ahead during cruise ship days.
addressDescription: Sandy Bay main road

name: Reef Rider Jet Ski Rentals
description: Jet ski rentals on West Bay Beach. Guided tours and free riding options available.
insiderTip: The guided tour takes you to a hidden beach.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, south end water sports area

name: Island Bicycle Rental
description: Mountain bikes and beach cruisers for rent. Great way to explore West End and West Bay.
insiderTip: The ride between West End and West Bay is mostly flat.
addressDescription: West End, near the south entrance

name: Dive Gear Rental Hub
description: Rent dive equipment without booking a dive shop. BCD, regs, wetsuits, and cameras available.
insiderTip: Their GoPro rentals come with a red filter.
addressDescription: West End, near the dive shops

name: Roatan Taxi Service
description: Reliable taxi service covering the whole island. Set prices, no meters. Call or WhatsApp for pickup.
insiderTip: Agree on the price before getting in.
addressDescription: Based in Coxen Hole, serves entire island

name: West Bay Water Taxi
description: Quick and scenic water taxi between West End and West Bay. Beats the road every time.
insiderTip: Last boat back is usually around 5pm.
addressDescription: West End main dock

name: Cruise Port Shuttle Express
description: Shuttle service from both cruise ports to West Bay, West End, and other destinations. Air conditioned.
insiderTip: Book the return shuttle when you arrive.
addressDescription: Mahogany Bay and Town Center cruise ports

name: Roatan Airport Transfers
description: Private airport pickup and dropoff service. Driver meets you at arrivals with your name on a sign.
insiderTip: Book 24 hours in advance for guaranteed service.
addressDescription: Juan Manuel Galvez Airport

name: East End Colectivo
description: Local minibus running from Coxen Hole to Oak Ridge and Punta Gorda. The cheapest way to travel.
insiderTip: Wave it down from any point along the main road.
addressDescription: Starts at Coxen Hole bus stop

name: Oak Ridge Water Taxi
description: Small boat service connecting Oak Ridge to Jonesville and surrounding communities. A local experience.
insiderTip: Ask to stop at the Hole in the Wall restaurant.
addressDescription: Oak Ridge main dock

name: VIP Transport Roatan
description: Luxury SUV service for private island tours and transfers. Professional English-speaking drivers.
insiderTip: They offer custom full-day island tours.
addressDescription: Serves entire island, based in West Bay

name: Roatan Ferry Terminal
description: Ferry service to La Ceiba on the mainland. Galaxy Wave catamaran runs daily schedules.
insiderTip: Buy tickets a day early during holidays.
addressDescription: Dixon Cove, between Coxen Hole and French Harbour

name: West Bay Beach
description: Roatan's most famous beach with powdery white sand and crystal clear water. The island's crown jewel.
insiderTip: Walk to the far south end to escape the cruise crowds.
addressDescription: West Bay, main beach access

name: Half Moon Bay Beach
description: Protected cove in West End, perfect for swimming and snorkeling. Calmer water than West Bay.
insiderTip: Great snorkeling right off the shore.
addressDescription: West End, follow signs to Half Moon Bay

name: Camp Bay Beach
description: Remote, uncrowded beach on Roatan's east end. Miles of sand and hardly anyone around.
insiderTip: Bring your own food and drinks, there are few vendors.
addressDescription: End of the road on the east end

name: Tabyana Beach Club
description: Popular beach club on West Bay with chair rentals, food, drinks, and water sports. Great for cruise visitors.
insiderTip: The all-inclusive day pass is the best deal.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, north section

name: Sandy Bay Beach
description: Quiet residential beach great for morning walks and kayaking. Less touristy than West Bay.
insiderTip: Visit the Carambola Botanical Gardens nearby.
addressDescription: Sandy Bay waterfront, multiple access points

name: Punta Gorda Beach
description: Garifuna community beach on the north shore. Cultural experience with traditional food vendors.
insiderTip: Try the pan de coco from the beach vendors.
addressDescription: Punta Gorda community waterfront

name: Big Bight Beach
description: Hidden beach near Flowers Bay with calm turquoise water. A local secret rarely visited by tourists.
insiderTip: 4x4 recommended for the access road.
addressDescription: Flowers Bay, rough road to the beach

name: Pristine Bay Beach
description: Exclusive beach at the Pristine Bay resort. Day passes available for non-guests.
insiderTip: Day passes sell out on cruise ship days.
addressDescription: Pristine Bay development, French Harbour area

name: Blue Marlin Night Club
description: Roatan's biggest nightclub with DJs, dance floor, and late-night energy. The weekend destination.
insiderTip: Things don't get going until after 11pm.
addressDescription: West End main strip

name: Sundowners Live Music Nights
description: Live bands playing reggae, rock, and Caribbean music on the waterfront. Best live music on the island.
insiderTip: Tuesday and Saturday are the best nights.
addressDescription: Sundowners Bar, West End waterfront

name: La Palapa Karaoke Bar
description: Popular karaoke bar where everyone joins in. Fun, silly, and surprisingly good singers some nights.
insiderTip: Get there early to sign up for popular songs.
addressDescription: West End, near Half Moon Bay

name: West Bay Beach Bonfire
description: Weekly beach bonfire events with fire dancers, drinks, and music under the stars. Magical atmosphere.
insiderTip: Bring a blanket to sit on the sand.
addressDescription: West Bay Beach, center section

name: Herby's Sports Bar
description: Watch live sports on big screens while enjoying cold beers and wings. Popular with expats.
insiderTip: NFL Sundays are packed - arrive early.
addressDescription: French Harbour main road

name: Coco Lounge
description: Chic lounge bar in West Bay with craft cocktails and chill house music. Upscale nightlife option.
insiderTip: The espresso martini is excellent.
addressDescription: West Bay, near the resorts

name: Roots Reggae Bar
description: Authentic reggae bar with Garifuna drumming nights. The most Caribbean atmosphere on the island.
insiderTip: Thursday is Garifuna drum night.
addressDescription: West End, north section
